headline: Hezbollah Downs Israeli Drone as Iran Fortifies Nuclear Sites, Ukraine Faces Multi‑Front UAV Surge
subheadline: Israel retaliated with airstrikes on southern Lebanese villages while Iran sealed uranium tunnels; Ukraine's air defenses shot down a hundred drones amid rising Russian bombings
lede: Hezbollah shot down an Israeli drone over the Bekaa Valley, prompting Israel to strike villages near Nabatieh and issue evacuation warnings. Iran intensified security around its enriched uranium stores by mining and sealing tunnel entrances. Qatar's secret talks with Tehran failed to stop a missile strike on the Ras Laffan gas complex. In Ukraine, multiple UAV groups moved from the Black Sea and Belarus toward Odessa, Kharkiv and Kyiv, while Russian bombers dropped heavy FAB‑250 bombs on Sloviansk.

sections.title: Ukrainian Front Pressed by UAV Swarms
sections.body: New UAV formations from the Black Sea are heading toward Odessa, targeting Zatoka, Serhiivka and Vylkove. A reactive UAV crossed from Belarus into the northern Kyiv region. Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 109 UAVs over Russian territory and the Black Sea. Russian tactical aviation activity increased on the eastern axis, and three 250‑kg FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, damaging over 20 apartment blocks.

sections.title: Lebanon Border Escalates Amid Iranian Nuclear Safeguards
sections.body: Hezbollah downed an Israeli drone in the Bekaa Valley, after which Israel bombed villages near Nabatieh and issued evacuation warnings. The IDF intercepted a suspicious aerial target in southern Lebanon with no casualties. Israeli forces killed seven Hezbollah militants in underground tunnels, uncovering weapons and launch sites. Iran is mining around enriched uranium storage sites and sealing tunnel entrances, while demanding the removal of foreign military bases.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The drone downing and swift Israeli retaliation raise the risk of rapid escalation along the Israel‑Lebanon frontier. Iran's tunnel‑sealing operations suggest a tightening of nuclear material security, potentially complicating diplomatic negotiations. Ukraine faces sustained multi‑directional UAV pressure combined with renewed Russian heavy‑bomb strikes, indicating an intensifying air‑war dimension on both fronts.
